title: Braille letters - EEG
description: This dataset contains raw EEG recordings from participants performing a tactile discrimination task involving Braille letter stimulation. Participants received Braille letters (B, C, D, L, M, N, V, Z) presented on Braille cells under their left and right index fingers while EEG was recorded. The dataset accompanies research investigating the neural transformation of sensory tactile input to perceptual representations of Braille letters in the visually deprived brain.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded using BrainVision equipment and stored in BrainVision format (.eeg, .vhdr, .vmrk files). Stimuli consisted of Braille letters presented tactilely to the left and right index fingers. Experimental triggers marked letter presentations (S1-8 for left hand, S9-16 for right hand), trial onsets, stimulus onsets, catch trials, and behavioral responses. Some participants completed multiple sessions, and recording was restarted within sessions for some participants, resulting in multiple runs.
